title: EventLedger replay reconstruction primitive (checkpoint + delta replay)
goal: Implement the deterministic state reconstruction primitive that, given a latest SessionCheckpoint, replays EventLedger events from the checkpoint's last_event_ledger_seq watermark forward to produce the current authoritative state. Spec §5 line 1208: 'replay still works when Flight Recorder or provider trace history is unavailable and EventLedger rows remain intact.'
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/state_replay.rs with `StateReplayer`, `ReplayPlan`, `ReplayResult`, `ReplayError` types; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/state_replay_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-190 SessionCheckpoint primitive merged (provides the watermark schema); KERNEL-001 EventLedger already in main.
implementation_notes: Per spec §5 line 1192-1208: 'Kernel V1 replay and promotion authority MUST come from the Postgres EventLedger ... replay still works when Flight Recorder or provider trace history is unavailable and EventLedger rows remain intact.' `StateReplayer::plan(session_id) -> ReplayPlan` does: (1) load latest SessionCheckpoint via watermark query (MT-190 index); (2) query KERNEL-001 EventLedger for events where seq > checkpoint.last_event_ledger_seq AND session_id matches, ordered by seq ASC; (3) return `ReplayPlan { from_checkpoint: SessionCheckpoint, events_to_replay: Vec<EventLedgerRow>, expected_final_seq: i64 }`. `StateReplayer::execute(plan, state_apply: impl Fn(&mut State, &EventLedgerRow) -> Result<(), ReplayError>) -> ReplayResult` applies events one-by-one to a state seeded from checkpoint.compact_state, returning either `Ok(final_state, final_seq)` or `Err(ReplayError { at_seq, cause })`. Determinism: events MUST be replayed in seq order; replay is idempotent over any single event (state_apply must be implemented so re-applying the same event is a no-op, but the replayer itself does not enforce this — it documents the contract). ReplayError variants: `EventNotApplicable { seq, reason }`, `StateInvariantViolated { seq, invariant }`, `MissingEvent { gap_at_seq }` (gap detection: assert no seq skipped between checkpoint.watermark+1 and final). The replayer logs every applied event count + final state hash via FR-EVT-REPLAY-{STARTED,PROGRESS,COMPLETED,FAILED}. No I/O during state_apply — the applicator function is pure (replayer takes &mut State, passes events). Tests: (a) checkpoint + 10 events -> replay produces expected final state; (b) gap detection (manually insert seq+2 skipping seq+1) -> returns MissingEvent; (c) state_apply error at event 5 -> ReplayError with at_seq=5; (d) determinism: 100 replays of same plan produce byte-identical final state.
risk_if_missed: Without deterministic replay, crash recovery cannot rebuild state authoritatively; spec §5 line 1208 invariant (replay works from EventLedger alone) is violated; the cluster X 'idempotent recovery' guarantee has no implementation.
validator_focus: Strict seq ordering (no in-memory re-sort allowed — query must be ORDER BY seq ASC); gap detection mandatory (silent gap = silent state loss); 100x determinism test asserts byte-identical replay output.

implementation_record.summary: MT-192 EventLedger replay reconstruction primitive (checkpoint + delta replay) verified against the folded cluster X.3 implementation at src/backend/handshake_core/src/session_checkpoint/replay.rs (PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT inherited from MT-190 — contract names src/process_ledger/state_replay.rs but the folded module landed under session_checkpoint/* alongside MT-190/191/193/194/195). The 2026-05-23 RFV session added 9 pure-Rust adversarial tests plus 1 #[ignore]-gated Postgres test. The 2026-05-25 post-ready hardening session patched replay.rs with StateReplayer::execute_with_flight_recorder, emitting best-effort FR-EVT-REPLAY-STARTED/PROGRESS/COMPLETED/FAILED receipts while preserving the primary replay result/error.
implementation_record.contract_deviation_note: MT-192 expected_diff_shape and owned_files name src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/state_replay.rs and tests/state_replay_tests.rs, but the folded implementation lives at src/session_checkpoint/replay.rs and the test surface lives at tests/session_checkpoint_tests.rs (shared). Same PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT class noted on MT-190 and MT-191. Operator/Integration Validator decision required.

test_results.note: test result: ok. 31 passed; 0 failed; 6 ignored. Of the 31 passing, 9 are MT-192 adversarial pure-Rust tests (mt_192_adversarial::*) and 2 are the pre-existing mt_192_replay_* tests. The 1 ignored MT-192 test under mt_191_192_adversarial_postgres::* requires POSTGRES_TEST_URL. Build wall-clock ~10m due to cold rebuild after concurrent sibling session lib changes.
Test file is rustfmt-clean after applying formatter to the appended MT-191 + MT-192 adversarial sections.

gap_detection_returns_typed_missing_even.in_memory_test_at_watermark: mt_192_adversarial::mt_192_adversarial_gap_at_watermark_boundary_returns_missing_event (checkpoint watermark=5; first event seq=7; expects MissingEvent { gap_at_seq: 6 })

determinism_100x_byte_identical_final_st.in_memory_test: mt_192_adversarial::mt_192_adversarial_replay_is_deterministic_over_100_runs (6-event mixed-kind plan replayed 100 times; DefaultHasher over serialized final_state is identical across all 100 runs)

fr_evt_replay_emission_at_every_phase.note: 2026-05-25 post-ready hardening wired FR-EVT-REPLAY-{STARTED,PROGRESS,COMPLETED,FAILED} emission through StateReplayer::execute_with_flight_recorder. The legacy execute() path remains receipt-free for compatibility; receipt-requiring callers should use the FR-aware helper.

spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_1_no_deferred_live_escape: PASS — no LiveXxxUnavailable / TODO / unimplemented paths in the new MT-192 adversarial test surface; all 9 pure-Rust tests exercise real StateReplayer code. The Postgres-gated test panics with ENVIRONMENT_BLOCKED prefix when run without POSTGRES_TEST_URL.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_2_external_resource_touch: PARTIAL — pure-Rust tests fully cover the type-level guarantees (9 PASS). The 1 Postgres-gated test for end-to-end checkpoint-write-then-replay against the real kernel_session_checkpoint table will only touch real Postgres when POSTGRES_TEST_URL is set. Same gate as MT-190/MT-191.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_3_implementer_cannot_self_certi: STRUCTURAL — lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260523-031814-AGT-X3-WR transitions PENDING -> CLAIMED -> READY_FOR_VALIDATION only. The READY_FOR_VALIDATION -> COMPLETED transition is reserved for the validator role.

edits_made_outside_owned_files: src/backend/handshake_core/src/flight_recorder/fr_emitter.rs: see MT-191 implementation_record.out_of_scope_self_evaluation for the rationale and exact scope. The same unblock fix served both MT-191 and MT-192 — they share the same test binary and lib dependency.
edits_made_inside_owned_files: src/backend/handshake_core/tests/session_checkpoint_tests.rs: appended a `mod mt_192_adversarial` module with 9 pure-Rust tests adjacent to the MT-191 module + 1 #[ignore]-gated Postgres test inside the shared `mod mt_191_192_adversarial_postgres`. Existing pre-MT-192-session tests preserved verbatim. rustfmt-clean.
out_of_scope_self_evaluation.implementation_files_intentionally_uncha: The contract's two owned files (src/process_ledger/state_replay.rs and tests/state_replay_tests.rs) do not exist at the contract-declared paths because the folded implementation landed at src/session_checkpoint/replay.rs and tests/session_checkpoint_tests.rs. Same operator decision required as MT-190/MT-191.

post_ready_hardening_20260525.base_scope: Close the MT-192 FR replay observability residual without changing READY_FOR_VALIDATION lifecycle authority.
product_changes: StateReplayer::execute_with_flight_recorder emits best-effort FR-EVT-REPLAY-STARTED before replay begins.
The FR-aware helper emits FR-EVT-REPLAY-PROGRESS after each applied event.
The FR-aware helper emits FR-EVT-REPLAY-COMPLETED after successful replay and FR-EVT-REPLAY-FAILED on missing-event or applicator failure.
Recorder failures are best-effort and do not mask ReplayResult or ReplayError.
high_roi_additions: Kept execute() unchanged for existing callers while adding an explicit receipt-producing path for restart/recovery call sites.
Reused the existing FlightRecorderEvent shape and replay plan/session identifiers for deterministic audit receipts.
Added both success and failure FR tests so later replay changes cannot silently drop terminal receipts.
gaps_closed: Previously documented FR-EVT-REPLAY-* emission was not executable in replay.rs.
Replay failure observability existed only as typed ReplayError serialization, not as a recordable FR event path.
No test verified that FR progress/completion order followed replay order.

37 passed; 0 failed; 6 ignored gated on POSTGRES_TEST_URL.
No SQLite/DuckDB references in touched checkpoint/replay files.

verification_needs_remaining: Run ignored Postgres-gated tests with POSTGRES_TEST_URL to exercise real checkpoint persistence and replay from persisted rows.
Integration Validator still needs to review the MT; lifecycle.integration_validator_verdict remains PENDING.
Audit restart/resume and recovery call sites to use execute_with_flight_recorder where FR receipts are required.

residual_risks: Postgres-backed adversarial test (1) remains #[ignore]-gated because POSTGRES_TEST_URL was unset in this session. Same gate as MT-190/MT-191.
PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT vs contract owned_files (process_ledger/state_replay.rs vs session_checkpoint/replay.rs). Same defect class as MT-190/MT-191; operator decision needed.
Out-of-scope edit to src/flight_recorder/fr_emitter.rs to unblock lib compilation — operator waiver requested (same as MT-191).

FR-aware replay telemetry is implemented on StateReplayer::execute_with_flight_recorder(); legacy StateReplayer::execute() remains receipt-free for compatibility. Validator should audit restart/resume call sites and migrate receipt-requiring paths to the FR-aware helper.

StateReplayer::plan re-sorts the events slice by seq ASC defensively, but the contract validator_focus explicitly says 'query must be ORDER BY seq ASC' — the in-memory re-sort is a defense-in-depth shim, not a replacement for the SQL ORDER BY at the caller. Recommend the caller's query is audited at integration time to ensure ORDER BY seq ASC is present.
No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-192; lifecycle.integration_validator_verdict remains PENDING.
next_dependent_mts_unblocked: MT-193 (restart resume orchestrator — depends on StateReplayer for event replay; orchestrator already wires this in the folded impl, MT-193 will validate)
MT-194 (idempotency ledger — depends on the watermark contract from MT-192's ReplayPlan to dedupe side-effects per session+seq)
MT-195 (integration tests for the whole cluster X.3 surface — depends on writer + replay both being RFV)

fixes.summary: Postgres ignored-test helpers now create isolated schemas through a direct connection and reconnect pools with options=-csearch_path%3D<schema>, preventing pooled-connection search_path drift.
fixes.gap_closed: Fixed MT-190/191/192 live Postgres failures where SET search_path on a PgPool made schema/table visibility nondeterministic.
